translations.View your time entries in a calendar for: View your time entries in a calendar format
translations.No time entries for this day: No time entries for this day
translations.This quick tour will help you get starte: This quick tour will help you get started with time tracking. You can skip this tour at any time.
translations.ArbeitszeitCheck helps you track your wo: ArbeitszeitCheck helps you track your working hours while ensuring compliance with German labor law (ArbZG) and GDPR requirements.
translations.Use the \"Clock In\" button to start tra: Use the "Clock In" button to start tracking your work time. When you finish, click "Clock Out" to stop.
translations.The system automatically tracks your wor: The system automatically tracks your working hours and ensures compliance with legal requirements.
translations.After 6 hours of work, you need at least: After 6 hours of work, you need at least 30 minutes of break time. After 9 hours, you need 45 minutes.
translations.Use \"Start Break\" and \"End Break\" to: Use "Start Break" and "End Break" to record your breaks. The system will remind you if you forget.
translations.The dashboard shows your daily, weekly,: The dashboard shows your daily, weekly, and monthly working hours, overtime balance, and vacation days remaining.
translations.You can also view your time entries in a: You can also view your time entries in a calendar or timeline format.
translations.You can request vacation days, sick leav: You can request vacation days, sick leave, or other absences through the Absences section.
translations.Your manager will review and approve or: Your manager will review and approve or reject your requests.
translations.Under GDPR, you have the right to access: Under GDPR, you have the right to access your personal data. You can export all your time tracking data at any time.
translations.Go to Settings to export your data in JS: Go to Settings to export your data in JSON format.
translations.You now know the basics of ArbeitszeitCh: You now know the basics of ArbeitszeitCheck. Start by clocking in to begin tracking your time!
translations.If you need help at any time, check the: If you need help at any time, check the documentation or contact your administrator.
translations.See your working hours and absences at a: See your working hours and absences at a glance. Click a day for details.
translations.No time entries or absences for this mon: No time entries or absences for this month.
translations.View and manage your time entries: View and manage your time entries
translations.View your time entries in calendar forma: View your time entries in calendar format
translations.View your time entries in timeline forma: View your time entries in timeline format
translations.Show or hide working time entries in the: Show or hide working time entries in the timeline
translations.Show or hide absence requests in the tim: Show or hide absence requests in the timeline
translations.Show or hide holidays in the timeline: Show or hide holidays in the timeline
translations.Select at least one type to display in t: Select at least one type to display in the timeline.
translations.All working time regulations are being f: All working time regulations are being followed correctly
translations.Some regulations may not be met. Click f: Some regulations may not be met. Click for details.
translations.Start tracking your time by clicking the: Start tracking your time by clicking the Clock In button above
translations.Overview of your working time and status: Overview of your working time and status
translations.Clock in to start tracking time: Clock in to start tracking time
translations.Clock out to end tracking time: Clock out to end tracking time
translations.You have worked {hours} hours today. Acc: You have worked {hours} hours today. According to German labor law, you must take a {minutes}-minute break. You still need {remaining} minutes of break time.
translations.Use these buttons to track your working: Use these buttons to track your working hours. Click "Clock In" when you start working and "Clock Out" when you finish.
translations.Click this button when you start working: Click this button when you start working. The system will track your working hours automatically.
translations.Click this button when you finish workin: Click this button when you finish working. The system will stop tracking your working hours.
translations.Click to start your break. German labor: Click to start your break. German labor law requires breaks after 6 hours of work.
translations.Click to end your break and resume worki: Click to end your break and resume working.
translations.Overview of your working hours, break ti: Overview of your working hours, break time, and overtime for today.
translations.Compliance status shows whether your wor: Compliance status shows whether your working hours follow German labor law (ArbZG) requirements. Click for more information.
translations.Maximum 8 hours of work per day (can be: Maximum 8 hours of work per day (can be extended to 10 hours in special cases)
translations.At least 30 minutes break after 6 hours: At least 30 minutes break after 6 hours of work
translations.At least 45 minutes break after 9 hours: At least 45 minutes break after 9 hours of work
translations.At least 11 hours of rest between shifts: At least 11 hours of rest between shifts
translations.Your current working status and today's: Your current working status and today's working hours.
Your most recent time tracking entries. Click on any entry to view or edit details.
translations.You can provide an optional reason for r: You can provide an optional reason for rejecting this request. This will be visible to the employee.
translations.Track your working hours legally and com: Track your working hours legally and compliantly

translations.Your recorded absences and their current: Your recorded absences and their current status
translations.No absences match your current filters: No absences match your current filters
translations.The following reason was provided for th: The following reason was provided for the rejection of this absence request:
translations.Request a new absence. Your manager will: Request a new absence. Your manager will review and approve or reject your request.
translations.Select the type of absence you want to r: Select the type of absence you want to request
translations.You can provide additional information a: You can provide additional information about your absence request
translations.Optional reason or notes for your absenc: Optional reason or notes for your absence request
translations.Generate and view detailed reports on wo: Generate and view detailed reports on working hours, overtime, and absences
translations.Start tracking time from the dashboard t: Start tracking time from the dashboard to see entries here
translations.Please explain why this correction is ne: Please explain why this correction is needed. This information will be reviewed by your supervisor.
translations.Please fill in all required fields: Please fill in all required fields
translations.Please fix compliance issues before subm: Please fix compliance issues before submitting
translations.See all important details for this absen: See all important details for this absence in one simple overview.
translations.Fill in the type, dates, and optional re: Fill in the type, dates, and optional reason and substitute.
translations.Set the actual last day of your absence: Set the actual last day of your absence so your records and your substitute's calendar stay accurate.
translations.Pick the day you actually returned. Must: Pick the day you actually returned. Must be before the original end date.
translations.Update end date and shorten absence: Update end date and shorten absence
translations.Select a colleague who will handle your: Select a colleague who will handle your tasks during your absence
translations.Select the type of report you want to ge: Select the type of report you want to generate. Each report type provides different insights into working hours, overtime, and absences.
translations.Select the date for the daily report: Select the date for the daily report
translations.Select the week start date (Monday) to g: Select the week start date (Monday) to generate a weekly report with daily breakdown.
translations.Select a month to generate a monthly rep: Select a month to generate a monthly report with user breakdown and statistics.
translations.Select a date range to analyze overtime: Select a date range to analyze overtime and undertime across all users.
translations.Select a date range to generate a report: Select a date range to generate a report on absences by type, status, and user.
translations.Select a date range and team member to g: Select a date range and team member to generate a comprehensive team report.
translations.Select the start date for the report per: Select the start date for the report period
translations.Select the end date for the report perio: Select the end date for the report period
translations.Select a team member to generate the tea: Select a team member to generate the team report. Multiple users can be added in future updates.
translations.Optionally select a specific user. Leave: Optionally select a specific user. Leave empty to include all users.
translations.Team reports require selecting a team me: Team reports require selecting a team member
translations.Remaining vacation days for this year. O: Remaining vacation days for this year. Only approved vacation (not sick leave or other absences) is deducted.
translations.Your absence history will appear here: Your absence history will appear here
translations.Absences: Send iCal by email after appro: Absences: Send iCal by email after approval
translations.When an absence is approved, the employe: When an absence is approved, the employee can receive an email with an iCalendar (.ics) attachment so they can add the absence to their calendar.
translations.Send iCal email to employee when absence: Send iCal email to employee when absence is approved
translations.Also send iCal to the substitute (if one: Also send iCal to the substitute (if one was selected)
translations.An absence has been approved. %1$s will: An absence has been approved. %1$s will be absent (%2$s) from %3$s to %4$s. An iCalendar file is attached so you can add it to your calendar.
translations.Your absence request (%1$s, %2$s – %3$s): Your absence request (%1$s, %2$s – %3$s) has been approved. An iCalendar file is attached so you can add it to your calendar.
translations.You have agreed to cover for %1$s (%2$s): You have agreed to cover for %1$s (%2$s) from %3$s to %4$s. An iCalendar file is attached so you can add it to your calendar.
translations.Review and approve or reject absence req: Review and approve or reject absence requests from your team.
Review and approve or reject absence requests and time entry corrections from your team.
translations.Error loading pending time entry correct: Error loading pending time entry corrections.
Failed to approve time entry correction.
Failed to reject time entry correction.
translations.Overview of working time compliance acro: Overview of working time compliance across your team.
translations.Some team members have compliance issues: Some team members have compliance issues. Check the Compliance section for details.
translations.You have been asked to cover for colleag: You have been asked to cover for colleagues during their absence. Approve or decline each request.
translations.When a colleague requests an absence and: When a colleague requests an absence and selects you as their substitute, you will see the request here.
translations.Optional reason for declining (leave emp: Optional reason for declining (leave empty for none):
translations.Edit to select a different substitute: Edit to select a different substitute
translations.You can edit to select a different subst: You can edit to select a different substitute or delete this request from the overview.
translations.Substitution requests: Approve or declin: Substitution requests: Approve or decline requests to cover for colleagues
translations.Absences: Email notifications for substi: Absences: Email notifications for substitution workflow
translations.When a substitute is selected, emails ca: When a substitute is selected, emails can be sent at each step of the approval process.
translations.Email substitute when a substitution req: Email substitute when a substitution request is created
translations.Email managers when substitute approves: Email managers when substitute approves (requires app teams)
translations.Substitution request: %1$s asks you to c: Substitution request: %1$s asks you to cover from %2$s to %3$s
translations.%1$s has requested you as their substitu: %1$s has requested you as their substitute for %2$s from %3$s to %4$s (%5$d day(s)). Please approve or decline this request.
translations.Substitute approved: %1$s will cover for: Substitute approved: %1$s will cover for you (%2$s – %3$s)
translations.%1$s has approved your substitution requ: %1$s has approved your substitution request (%2$s – %3$s). Your absence is now awaiting manager approval.
translations.%1$s has requested an absence (%2$s) fro: %1$s has requested an absence (%2$s) from %3$s to %4$s. The substitute %5$s has approved. Please review and approve or reject the request.
translations.Absence is not awaiting substitute appro: Absence is not awaiting substitute approval
translations.You are not the designated substitute fo: You are not the designated substitute for this absence
translations.Auto-approved: no manager assigned in yo: Auto-approved: no manager assigned in your team.
translations.Automatisch genehmigt: In Ihrem Team ist: Auto-approved: no manager assigned in your team.
translations.Optional reason for rejection (leave emp: Optional reason for rejection (leave empty for none):
translations.Do you really want to cancel this absenc: Do you really want to cancel this absence? This cannot be undone.
translations.Note: Team reports currently support sin: Note: Team reports currently support single user selection. Multiple users can be added in future updates.
translations.Monitor labor law compliance and violati: Monitor labor law compliance and violations
Please refresh the page to try again.
translations.Enter your working times including break: Enter your working times including breaks. The system will automatically check compliance with German labor law (ArbZG).
translations.Add all breaks you took during this work: Add all breaks you took during this working day. German labor law requires at least 30 minutes break after 6 hours, and 45 minutes after 9 hours of work.

translations.Statutory holidays are automatically res: Statutory holidays are automatically restored when the calendar is viewed, unless "Auto-restore statutory holidays" is disabled in Settings.

translations.Are you sure you want to delete \"{name}:
Are you sure you want to delete "{name}"?

This will permanently remove this work schedule. If any employees are using this schedule, you should assign them to a different schedule first.

This action cannot be undone.
translations.Manage statutory and additional holidays: Manage statutory and additional holidays per federal state. These calendars are used for vacation, working time calculations, and compliance checks.
translations.This federal state is used automatically: This federal state is used automatically when no own state is configured for employees or teams.
translations.The federal state for an employee is set: The federal state for an employee is set by administrators or managers, for example in %1$sEmployee settings%2$s. If no own state is configured there, the default state configured here is used.
translations.Select a federal state and year to view: Select a federal state and year to view and edit statutory holidays plus additional company or custom holidays.
translations.Do you really want to remove the holiday: Do you really want to remove the holiday "{name}" on {date}?
translations.End time must be after start time: End time must be after start time
translations.Break end time must be after break start: Break end time must be after break start time
translations.Break times must be within your working: Break times must be within your working hours
translations.German labor law (ArbZG) allows a maximu: German labor law (ArbZG) allows a maximum of 10 hours per day. You have entered {hours} hours.
translations.You have worked {hours} hours. The norma: You have worked {hours} hours. The normal maximum is 8 hours per day. Extended hours must be compensated within 6 months.
translations.German labor law requires at least 30 mi: German labor law requires at least 30 minutes break after 6 hours of work. You have only {minutes} minutes.
translations.You have worked {hours} hours. Make sure: You have worked {hours} hours. Make sure you have taken at least 30 minutes of break.
translations.German labor law requires at least 45 mi: German labor law requires at least 45 minutes break after 9 hours of work. You have only {minutes} minutes.
translations.Date, start time, and end time are requi: Date, start time, and end time are required
translations.Please fix the errors before saving: Please fix the errors before saving
translations.e.g., Project work, meeting preparation,: e.g., Project work, meeting preparation, etc.
translations.Edit your working times including breaks: Edit your working times including breaks. The system will automatically check compliance with German labor law (ArbZG).

translations.Optional description for this time entry: Optional description for this time entry
translations.View and manage all time entries for thi: View and manage all time entries for this day. You can edit entries or request corrections.

translations.Edit the working time model settings bel: Edit the working time model settings below. Changes will affect all users assigned to this model.

translations.Select a date range to view your time en: Select a date range to view your time entries in timeline format
translations.Reports for team and organisation summar: Reports: Summaries for teams and organisation (visible to managers and admins only)

translations.View your work periods in a chronologica: View your work periods in a chronological timeline
translations.Split overnight entries at midnight in C: Split overnight entries at midnight in CSV/JSON export
translations.When enabled, entries that run across mi: When enabled, entries that run across midnight (for example 22:00–06:00) are shown as two lines in the export (before and after 00:00). This is only a visual/export split – all internal working time and ArbZG compliance checks continue to use the original, unsplit entry.
translations.Select a date range and click Load Timel: Select a date range and click Load Timeline to view your work periods
translations.Add all breaks for the corrected time en: Add all breaks for the corrected time entry.
translations.Please explain why this correction is ne: Please explain why this correction is needed...
translations.Additional notes for the corrected entry: Additional notes for the corrected entry
translations.Working hours exceed the legal maximum o: Working hours exceed the legal maximum of 10 hours per day. This requires special approval.
translations.Working hours exceed the normal 8 hours: Working hours exceed the normal 8 hours per day. This requires special approval.
translations.After 9 hours of work, at least 45 minut: After 9 hours of work, at least 45 minutes of break time is required by German labor law (ArbZG).
translations.After 6 hours of work, at least 30 minut: After 6 hours of work, at least 30 minutes of break time is required by German labor law (ArbZG).

translations.Manage your personal preferences and not: Manage your personal preferences and notification settings
translations.Your working time model, vacation days,: Your working time model, vacation days, and working hours are assigned by your administrator. Contact your administrator if you have questions or need changes.
translations.This app helps you record your work time: This app helps you record your work time and follow German labor law. Here's how to get started:
translations.Click the \"Clock In\" button below when: Click the "Clock In" button below when you start work
translations.Click \"Clock Out\" when you finish work: Click "Clock Out" when you finish work
translations.The system will automatically track your: The system will automatically track your hours and remind you to take breaks
translations.You can also add time entries manually o: You can also add time entries manually or request vacation days in the "Absences" section
translations.Go to time entries to see how to add ent: Go to time entries to see how to add entries manually
translations.Click to hide this welcome message: Click to hide this welcome message
translations.See your current work status, today's ho: See your current work status, today's hours, and recent time entries
translations.Go to dashboard to see your work status: Go to dashboard to see your work status and today's hours
translations.Go to time entries to see all your worki: Go to time entries to see all your working time records
translations.Go to absences to request vacation or si: Go to absences to request vacation or sick leave
translations.Go to reports to create and download wor: Go to reports to create and download working time reports
translations.Go to settings to change your preference: Go to settings to change your preferences
translations.Manager: Approve absences and time corre: Manager: Approve absences and time corrections, see team overview
translations.Go to manager dashboard to approve reque: Go to manager dashboard to approve requests and see your team
translations.Track your working time and follow Germa: Track your working time and follow German labor law
translations.Dashboard: See your current work status,: Dashboard: See your current work status, today's hours, and recent time entries
translations.Time Entries: View, add, edit, or delete: Time Entries: View, add, edit, or delete all your working time records
translations.Absences: Request and manage vacation da: Absences: Request and manage vacation days, sick leave, and other time off
translations.Reports: Create and download reports abo: Reports: Create and download reports about your working time
translations.Calendar: View your working time and abs: Calendar: View your working time and absences in a calendar view
translations.Timeline: See your working time history: Timeline: See your working time history in chronological order
translations.Settings: Change your personal preferenc: Settings: Change your personal preferences and working time settings
translations.Go to calendar to see your working time: Go to calendar to see your working time in a calendar
translations.Go to timeline to see your working time: Go to timeline to see your working time history
translations.Set up how time tracking works for every: Set up how time tracking works for everyone in your organization
translations.An unexpected error occurred. Please try: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again. If the problem continues, contact your administrator.
translations.Checks working times immediately when sa: Checks working times immediately when saving or editing. Disable only if you run compliance checks exclusively via batch processing.
translations.In default mode, violations are shown bu: In default mode, violations are shown but saving is still possible. In strict mode, violations prevent saving the time entry.
translations.The system will automatically check if w: The system will automatically check if working hours follow German labor law. For example, it will warn if someone works more than 8 hours per day without a break.
translations.Require a reason when taking breaks: Require a reason when taking breaks
translations.When employees take breaks, they must wr: When employees take breaks, they must write a short reason. This helps ensure breaks are used correctly and comply with labor law.
translations.Send alerts when working time rules are: Send alerts when working time rules are broken
translations.When someone works too many hours or doe: When someone works too many hours or doesn't take required breaks, the system will send a notification to managers and the employee.
translations.The most hours someone can work in one d: The most hours someone can work in one day. German law allows up to 8 hours normally, or 10 hours in special cases. Example: Enter 10 to allow up to 10 hours per day.
translations.Minimum Rest Time Between Work Days: Minimum Rest Time Between Work Days
translations.How many hours of rest someone must have: How many hours of rest someone must have between the end of one work day and the start of the next. German law requires at least 11 hours. Example: If someone finishes work at 6 PM, they cannot start again before 5 AM the next day.
translations.The normal number of hours people work e: The normal number of hours people work each day. This is used for new employees until you set their individual working hours. Example: Enter 8 for a standard 8-hour workday.
translations.Select the German state where your organ: Select the German state where your organization is located. This ensures the system follows the correct labor laws for that state.
translations.How Long to Keep Time Records: How Long to Keep Time Records
translations.How many years to keep time tracking rec: How many years to keep time tracking records before they are automatically deleted. German law typically requires keeping records for 2 years. Example: Enter 2 to keep records for 2 years.
translations.Click to save all settings. Changes will: Click to save all settings. Changes will apply to all users.
translations.Cancel and go back to dashboard: Cancel and go back to dashboard
translations.Click to cancel and go back without savi: Click to cancel and go back without saving changes
translations.See how many employees are using the sys: See how many employees are using the system and check for any problems with working time rules
translations.Total number of employees who can use ti: Total number of employees who can use time tracking
translations.Number of employees who clocked in today: Number of employees who clocked in today
translations.Number of working time rule violations t: Number of working time rule violations that need attention
translations.Working time rule violations that need y: Working time rule violations that need your attention
translations.Great! All employees are following the w: Great! All employees are following the working time rules correctly.
translations.See how your team is doing with time tra: See how your team is doing with time tracking and check for any problems
translations.Check if your working time follows Germa: Check if your working time follows German labor law and see any problems that need fixing
translations.Your working time follows all German lab: Your working time follows all German labor law rules. Keep up the good work!
translations.There are some issues with your working: There are some issues with your working time that need attention. Please check the list below and fix them.
translations.How well you follow the rules:: How well you follow the rules:
translations.This shows how well your working time fo: This shows how well your working time follows German labor law. 100%% means everything is perfect.
translations.See statistics about working time proble: See statistics about working time problems and how well employees follow the rules
translations.Total number of times working time rules: Total number of times working time rules were broken
translations.Number of problems that still need to be: Number of problems that still need to be fixed
translations.See what kinds of working time problems: See what kinds of working time problems occurred
translations.See how serious the problems were: See how serious the problems were
translations.List of times when working time rules we: List of times when working time rules were not followed correctly
translations.See when working time rules were not fol: See when working time rules were not followed and what needs to be fixed
translations.Filter by how serious the problem is: Filter by how serious the problem is
translations.Choose to see only serious problems, min: Choose to see only serious problems, minor problems, or all problems
translations.Click to show only the problems that mat: Click to show only the problems that match your filter settings
translations.Great! No working time rule violations w: Great! No working time rule violations were found for the selected time period.
translations.See a record of all actions taken in the: See a record of all actions taken in the time tracking system
translations.Search by employee name or email address: Search by employee name or email address
translations.Search for activities by a specific empl: Search for activities by a specific employee
translations.Type an employee name to find employees: Type an employee name to find employees quickly
translations.Choose to see only certain types of acti: Choose to see only certain types of actions, like adding, changing, or deleting records
translations.Download the activity log as a file: Download the activity log as a file
translations.Click to download all activity records a: Click to download all activity records as a file for your records
translations.Assign working time models and set vacat: Assign working time models and set vacation days per year for each employee.
translations.Open employee management to assign worki: Open employee management to assign working time models and vacation entitlements
translations.Open working time models to define stand: Open working time models to define standard schedules
translations.Configure teams and managers for approva: Configure teams and managers for approvals and compliance responsibilities.
translations.Adjust global compliance rules, notifica: Adjust global compliance rules, notifications, and integration settings.
translations.Go to employees to assign working time m: Go to employees to assign working time models and vacation days
translations.No activity was recorded for the selecte: No activity was recorded for the selected time period.
translations.Add employees to time tracking and set t: Add employees to time tracking and set their work schedules
translations.Search employees by name or email addres: Search employees by name or email address
translations.Type a name or email address to find emp: Type a name or email address to find employees quickly
translations.Click to reload the list and see any new: Click to reload the list and see any new employees
translations.Click to change this employee's work sch: Click to change this employee's work schedule or other settings
translations.Set up different work schedules and assi: Set up different work schedules and assign them to employees
translations.Click to create a new work schedule. For: Click to create a new work schedule. For example, you could create a model for full-time employees (8 hours per day) or part-time employees (4 hours per day).
translations.Click to change the working hours or nam: Click to change the working hours or name of this work schedule
translations.Click to delete this work schedule. This: Click to delete this work schedule. This cannot be undone, so make sure no employees are using it first.
translations.Are you sure you want to delete this wor:
Are you sure you want to delete this work schedule?

This will permanently remove this schedule. If any employees are using this schedule, you should assign them to a different schedule first.

This action cannot be undone.
translations.Request time off for vacation or sick le: Request time off for vacation or sick leave
translations.Click to request time off. You can reque: Click to request time off. You can request vacation days, sick leave, or other types of absences.
translations.Delete this time entry permanently. This: Delete this time entry permanently. This cannot be undone.
translations.Are you sure you want to delete this tim:
Are you sure you want to delete this time entry?

This will permanently remove this record of your working time. This action cannot be undone.
translations.View working hours for a specific day: View working hours for a specific day
translations.Choose one of these exports, then set th: Choose one of these exports, then set the period and format below.
translations.Export a clear overview of worked hours: Export a clear overview of worked hours for the selected period.
translations.Export vacation and absence data with to: Export vacation and absence data with totals and status.
translations.Export labor law compliance violations a: Export labor law compliance violations and severity details.
translations.Pick the time period for your report. Th: Pick the time period for your report. The selected range applies to the export and preview.
Preview loads the report in the browser. Generate and download saves a file using the options below.
translations.Summary matches the on-screen team total: Summary (one row per person) matches the on-screen team totals; it is aggregated, so the midnight split does not apply. Detailed time entries lists each booking line and respects the midnight split when enabled in administration.
translations.Daily sheet: date, weekday, From–To pair: Daily sheet: date, weekday, From–To pairs (wide)
translations.Wide layout groups pairs of start and en: Wide layout groups pairs of start and end times per calendar day for spreadsheet review.
translations.Long layout: one row per time segment wi: Long layout: one row per time segment with start/end times and duration/working hours columns (use this to see overnight shifts split at midnight when your administrator enables it). Wide layout: date, weekday, and From–To clock times only — no duration or working hours columns.
translations.Example for CSV/JSON long layout: row 1: Example for CSV/JSON long layout: row 1 has date = first calendar day, start_time 22:00:00, end_time 23:59:59; row 2 has date = next day, start_time 00:00:00, end_time 06:00:00. Column working_hours is the work time share per segment (the segments sum to the full entry). This is not an extra "break" row — rest breaks remain tied to the original booking; split rows may show empty break columns.
translations.Downloads the last 30 days as CSV (long: Downloads the last 30 days as CSV (long layout): each row shows start and end times; overnight shifts appear as two rows when your administrator enables midnight split. Use Reports for date range and optional wide layout.
translations.Quick CSV uses the long layout (columns: Quick CSV uses the long layout (columns include start and end times). Overnight entries can appear as two rows after midnight if the administrator enabled split in export settings.
translations.DATEV export always uses full, unsplit t: DATEV export always uses full, unsplit time entries as required by the DATEV payroll format. CSV and JSON exports respect the midnight split setting above when it is enabled.
translations.What information do you want in the repo: What information do you want in the report?
The first day to include in the report. Click the calendar icon to pick a date. Example: If you want a report for January, select January 1st.
translations.The last day to include in the report. C: The last day to include in the report. Click the calendar icon to pick a date. Example: If you want a report for January, select January 31st.
Choose how you want to save the report. PDF is best for printing or viewing. Excel or CSV is best if you want to edit the data in a spreadsheet program.
Choose how you want to save the report. CSV works well with spreadsheet programs. JSON is best if another system needs to process the data.
translations.Click to see what the report will look l: Click to see what the report will look like before downloading it
translations.Click to create the report and download: Click to create the report and download it to your computer
translations.Click to cancel and go back without gene: Click to cancel and go back without generating a report
translations.Who should be included in the report?: Who should be included in the report?
translations.Includes all people you are responsible: Includes all people you are responsible for as a manager.
translations.This list is filled automatically if app: This list is filled automatically if app-owned teams are enabled.
translations.Please choose who should be included in: Please choose who should be included in the report.
translations.The download contains one row per team m: The download contains one row per team member for the selected period.
translations.The download lists each time entry line: The download lists each time entry line for your team (several rows per person if there are multiple entries; overnight shifts may appear as two rows when midnight split is enabled).
translations.Team download is only available for the: Team download is only available for the working time export. Switch to personal scope to download absence or compliance data.
translations.With team scope, this preview shows the: With team scope, this preview shows the team working time summary, not absence or compliance.
translations.Team file download is only available for: Team file download is only available for the working time export.
translations.Name: Name
translations.Pick the start and end date for your rep: Pick the start and end date for your report.
translations.Choose the kind of report you need. Then: Choose the kind of report you need. Then set the date range below.
translations.How many vacation days you get each year: How many vacation days you get each year. In Germany, the standard is 25 days. Example: Enter 25 if you get 25 vacation days per year.
translations.The system will automatically calculate: The system will automatically calculate when you need to take breaks according to German labor law. For example, if you work more than 6 hours, you must take at least a 30-minute break.
translations.Click to save your working time preferen: Click to save your working time preferences
translations.Your recent time entries will appear her: Your recent time entries will appear here. Start by clocking in to track your working time.
translations.fieldRequired: This field is required. Please fill in this field.
translations.emailInvalid: Please enter a valid email address. Example: name@example.com
translations.minLength: Please enter at least {min} characters. You entered {current} characters.
translations.maxLength: Please enter no more than {max} characters. You entered {current} characters.
translations.invalidFormat: The format is incorrect. Please check the format and try again.
translations.numberInvalid: Please enter a valid number. Example: {example}
translations.numberMin: Please enter a number that is at least {min}. You entered {current}.
translations.numberMax: Please enter a number that is no more than {max}. You entered {current}.
translations.timeInvalid: Please enter the time in this format: HH:MM (hours:minutes). Example: 09:00 for 9 in the morning, or 14:30 for 2:30 in the afternoon.
translations.dateInvalid: Please enter a valid date. Click the calendar icon to pick a date, or enter it in this format: YYYY-MM-DD. Example: 2024-01-15 for January 15, 2024.
translations.unexpectedError: Something went wrong. Please check your internet connection and try again. If the problem continues, contact support.
translations.Click to open or close the navigation me: Click to open or close the navigation menu
translations.Enter a name for this work schedule (e.g: Enter a name for this work schedule (e.g., "Full-Time", "Part-Time")
translations.Select a work schedule to assign to this: Select a work schedule to assign to this employee
translations.Number of vacation days per year (standa: Number of vacation days per year (standard in Germany: 25 days)
translations.Leave empty if the assignment has no end: Leave empty if the assignment has no end date
translations.Decimal hours are allowed (e.g. 38.7 h/w: Decimal hours are allowed (e.g. 38.7 h/week under a collective agreement). Overtime targets use this weekly total spread across your working days.
translations.Usual hours per workday (e.g. 7.74 if 38: Usual hours per workday (e.g. 7.74 if 38.7 h/week over five days). Overtime compares worked time to the weekly contract hours × (working days ÷ 5).
translations.Default daily working hours. Used for ne: Default daily working hours. Used for new employees until individual models are set. Decimal hours are allowed (e.g. 7.7).
translations.How many hours you normally work each da: How many hours you normally work each day. Decimal values are allowed (e.g. 7.74 for a 38.7 h week spread over five days).
translations.Remaining vacation days for this year (a: Remaining vacation days for this year (annual entitlement plus carryover minus approved vacation). Sick leave and other absences are not deducted.
translations.Opening balance of carryover days for th: Opening balance of carryover days for the selected calendar year (Resturlaub), e.g. from HR or migration. This is not the annual vacation entitlement from the working time model. The last day carryover can be used is set globally in Admin settings.
translations.The calendar year this opening balance b: The calendar year this opening balance belongs to—the same year shown in vacation statistics. Use the year you are booking against (often the current year).
translations.The calendar year this opening balance a: The calendar year this opening balance applies to (same year as in employees’ vacation statistics—usually the current year). When a new year starts or after migrating from another system, set the Resturlaub opening balance for that year here or use the CSV import command; the app does not roll balances forward automatically.
translations.Vacation carryover must be between 0 and: Vacation carryover must be between 0 and 366 days
translations.Break start time cannot be before work s: Break start time cannot be before work start time
translations.Break end time cannot be after work end: Break end time cannot be after work end time
translations.Justification is required for manual tim: Justification is required for manual time entries
translations.End date must be after start date: End date must be after start date
translations.Weekly hours must be greater than 0: Weekly hours must be greater than 0
translations.Daily hours must be greater than 0: Daily hours must be greater than 0
translations.Valid working time model ID is required: Valid working time model ID is required
translations.Access denied. You can only approve abse: Access denied. You can only approve absences for members of your team.
translations.Access denied. You can only reject absen: Access denied. You can only reject absences for members of your team.
translations.Access denied. You can only view reports: Access denied. You can only view reports for yourself or your team members.
translations.Access denied. You can only view complia: Access denied. You can only view compliance data for yourself or your team members.
translations.Substitute must be an existing user: Substitute must be an existing user
translations.Substitute must be a colleague in your t: Substitute must be a colleague in your team. Please select someone who shares a team or group with you.
translations.A substitute is required for this absenc: A substitute is required for this absence type. Please select who will cover for you.
translations.Select who will cover for you (optional): Select who will cover for you (optional)
translations.Optionally choose a colleague who will c: Optionally choose a colleague who will cover your tasks during your absence.
translations.Choose a colleague from your team who wi: Choose a colleague from your team who will cover your tasks during your absence. Only team members appear in this list.
translations.No team members found. Add yourself to a: No team members found. Add yourself to a team or group to select a substitute.
translations.Could not load team members. Please try: Could not load team members. Please try again.
translations.Time entry is not pending approval: Time entry is not pending approval
translations.Access denied - user is not in your team: Access denied - user is not in your team
translations.Either (date and hours) or (startTime an: Either (date and hours) or (startTime and endTime) are required
translations.Invalid start or end time format. Use IS: Invalid start or end time format. Use ISO 8601 (e.g. Y-m-dTH:i:s).
Invalid break start or end time format.
translations.Type, start_date, and end_date are requi: Type, start date, and end date are required
translations.Justification is required for correction: Justification is required for correction requests
translations.Time entry has no start time: Time entry has no start time
translations.Correction request submitted and auto-ap: Correction request submitted and auto-approved (no manager in your team).
translations.Cannot delete working time model: %d use: Cannot delete working time model: %d user(s) are assigned to this model. Please reassign users first.
translations.Maximum daily hours must be between 1 an: Maximum daily hours must be between 1 and 24
translations.Minimum rest period must be between 1 an: Minimum rest period must be between 1 and 24 hours
translations.Failed to encode data as JSON: Failed to encode data as JSON
translations.Export date range must not exceed %d day: Export date range must not exceed %d days. Please narrow the range.
translations.Date range must not exceed %d days. Plea: Date range must not exceed %d days. Please narrow the range.
translations.Project ID must not exceed %d characters: Project ID must not exceed %d characters
translations.User IDs must be provided for team repor: User IDs must be provided for team reports
translations.No users to include in the report. Pleas: No users to include in the report. Please select a scope or team.
translations.Access denied. You can only view reports: Access denied. You can only view reports for teams you manage.
translations.Start date must be before or equal to en: Start date must be before or equal to end date.
Start date must be before or equal to end date
translations.Export for team or organization scope is: The download will contain one row per team member matching this preview.
translations.Export for organization scope is not yet: Export for organization scope is not yet available. Use Preview to view the report.
translations.Start date and end date are required for: Start date and end date are required for custom period
translations.Invalid date format. Expected yyyy-mm-dd: Invalid date format. Expected yyyy-mm-dd or dd.mm.yyyy: %s
translations.Failed to load violations. Please try ag: Failed to load violations. Please try again.
translations.Your compliance score is low. No specifi: Your compliance score is low. No specific violations have been recorded in the recent period. This may be due to missing time entries or data that has not been analyzed yet.
Only manual time entries can be deleted. Automatic entries cannot be deleted.
translations.This entry has a pending correction requ: This entry has a pending correction request. Deleting it may affect the approval process.
translations.Filter absence requests by date or statu: Filter absence requests by date or status
translations.Click to show options for filtering your: Click to show options for filtering your absence requests. You can filter by date range or approval status.
translations.Failed to load working time models: Failed to load working time models
translations.Are you sure you want to cancel this abs: Are you sure you want to cancel this absence request?
translations.The first day of your absence: The first day of your absence
translations.The last day of your absence: The last day of your absence
translations.End date cannot be before start date: End date cannot be before start date
translations.Start date cannot be after end date: Start date cannot be after end date
translations.Not enough vacation days remaining. You: Not enough vacation days remaining. You have %1$s days left for %2$s but requested %3$s days.
translations.Sick leave start date cannot be more tha: Sick leave start date cannot be more than %s days in the past.
translations.This period overlaps with an existing %1: This period overlaps with an existing %1$s (%2$s – %3$s).
translations.If you were sick during vacation, please: If you were sick during vacation, please shorten or cancel the vacation first, then submit a separate sick leave request.
translations.Date is required and cannot be empty: Date is required and cannot be empty
translations.Vacation must include at least one worki: Vacation must include at least one working day. The selected period contains only weekends or public holidays.
translations.Break must be within work period: Break must be within work period
translations.Break must be at least 15 minutes long t: Break must be at least 15 minutes long to count toward legal break requirement (ArbZG §4). Current duration: %d minutes
translations.Warning: 6-month average working hours (: Warning: 6-month average working hours (%.2f h/day) exceeds 8 hours/day. 10-hour days are only allowed if the average does not exceed 8 hours (ArbZG §3).
translations.Warning: 6-month average weekly working: Warning: 6-month average weekly working hours (%.2f h/week) exceeds 48 hours/week (ArbZG §3).
translations.Record when you worked by entering the s: Record when you worked by entering the start and end times, and any breaks you took.
translations.Edit your time entry. You can only edit: Edit your time entry. You can only edit manual entries or entries with pending approval.
translations.Edit your time entry. You can edit manua: Edit your time entry. You can edit manual entries, entries with pending approval, or completed automatic entries.
Edit your time entry. You can edit manual entries, entries with pending approval, or completed automatic entries from the last 2 weeks.
translations.Cannot edit this time entry. Please use: Cannot edit this time entry. Please use "Request Correction" for approved entries.
translations.Cannot edit this time entry. Only entrie: Cannot edit this time entry. Only entries from the last 2 weeks can be edited.
translations.Optional: When did your break start?: Optional: When did your break start?
translations.Optional: When did your break end?: Optional: When did your break end?
translations.Optional description or notes about this: Optional description or notes about this work period
translations.Optional: Add notes or details about thi: Optional: Add notes or details about this work period (e.g., project work, meetings, tasks)
translations.What time did you start working?: What time did you start working?
translations.What time did you finish working?: What time did you finish working?
You have not requested any absences yet. Use the button below to request vacation, sick leave, or other time off.
translations.Reports are only available for administr: Reports are only available for administrators and managers
translations.If you need to generate reports, please: If you need to generate reports, please contact your administrator or manager.
translations.Maximum working time: 8 hours per day (c: Maximum working time: 8 hours per day (can be extended to 10 hours)
translations.Minimum rest period: 11 hours between wo: Minimum rest period: 11 hours between working days
translations.Minimum 11-hour rest period required bet: Minimum 11-hour rest period required between shifts (ArbZG §5). Your last shift ended on %s at %s. This entry cannot start before %s (%.1f hours required).
translations.Mandatory breaks: 30 min after 6 hours,: Mandatory breaks: 30 min after 6 hours, 45 min after 9 hours
translations.Sunday work is generally prohibited with: Sunday work is generally prohibited with exceptions
translations.Export your personal data in accordance: Export your personal data in accordance with GDPR
translations.German labor law compliant time tracking: German labor law compliant time tracking for Nextcloud
translations.Get a notification when it's time to tak: Get a notification when it's time to take a required break. For example, if you work more than 6 hours, you'll get a reminder to take at least a 30-minute break.
translations.Optional: Record your break times. Break: Optional: Record your break times. Breaks of at least 15 minutes count toward legal requirements (ArbZG §4).
Optional: Record your break times. Breaks of at least 15 minutes count toward legal requirements (ArbZG §4). You can add multiple breaks.
translations.Select the day you worked (format: dd.mm: Select the day you worked (format: dd.mm.yyyy)
translations.Working hours automatically limited to %: Working hours automatically limited to %1$s hours (ArbZG §3). End time adjusted from %2$s to %3$s.
translations.Working hours (excluding breaks) exceed: Working hours (excluding breaks) exceed the legal maximum of
translations.Note: You are approaching the maximum wo: Note: You are approaching the maximum working hours. Extended hours must be compensated within 6 months (ArbZG §3).
translations.Break must be at least 15 minutes long t: Break must be at least 15 minutes long to count toward legal break requirement (ArbZG §4). Current duration:
translations.You haven't recorded any working time ye: You haven't recorded any working time yet. Click the button below to add your first time entry, or use the clock in button on the dashboard to start tracking automatically.
translations.Are you sure you want to delete this tim: Are you sure you want to delete this time entry?
translations.Add a new time entry to record when you: Add a new time entry to record when you worked
translations.Click to add a new time entry. You can r: Click to add a new time entry. You can record when you started and finished work, and any breaks you took.
translations.Filter time entries by date or status: Filter time entries by date or status
translations.Click to show options for filtering your: Click to show options for filtering your time entries. You can filter by date range or status.
translations.Download your time entries as a file: Download your time entries as a file
translations.Click to download all your time entries: Click to download all your time entries as a file. You can choose PDF, Excel, or CSV format.
translations.This time entry overlaps with existing e: This time entry overlaps with existing entries: %s
Use this form to record when you worked. Fill in the date, start time, end time, and optionally break times. The form will automatically check compliance with German labor law requirements.
translations.Use this form to edit your time entry. T: Use this form to edit your time entry. The form will automatically check compliance with German labor law requirements.
translations.No breaks required for shifts under 6 ho: No breaks required for shifts under 6 hours
translations.Click to save your time entry. The form: Click to save your time entry. The form will be validated before saving.
translations.Click to update your time entry. The for: Click to update your time entry. The form will be validated before saving.
translations.Cancel and return to time entries list: Cancel and return to time entries list
translations.Date is required. Please enter a date in: Date is required. Please enter a date in the format dd.mm.yyyy (e.g., 15.01.2024).
translations.Invalid date format. Please use dd.mm.yy: Invalid date format. Please use dd.mm.yyyy (e.g., 15.01.2024).
translations.Year must be between 1900 and 2100. You: Year must be between 1900 and 2100. You entered %s.
translations.Month must be between 1 and 12. You ente: Month must be between 1 and 12. You entered %s.
translations.Invalid day for %s/%s. This month has %s: Invalid day for %s/%s. This month has %s days.
translations.Invalid date: %s. Please check and try a: Invalid date: %s. Please check and try again.
translations.Future dates are not allowed. Please ent: Future dates are not allowed. Please enter a date today or in the past.
translations.Date is too far in the past. Maximum all: Date is too far in the past. Maximum allowed: %s years ago.
translations.Time is required. Please enter a time in: Time is required. Please enter a time in the format HH:MM (e.g., 09:00).
translations.Invalid time format. Please use HH:MM (2: Invalid time format. Please use HH:MM (24-hour format, e.g., 09:00 for 9 AM or 17:30 for 5:30 PM).
translations.Hour must be between 0 and 23. You enter: Hour must be between 0 and 23. You entered %s.
translations.Minute must be between 0 and 59. You ent: Minute must be between 0 and 59. You entered %s.
translations.Both break start and end times are requi: Both break start and end times are required if you enter a break.
translations.Break must be completely within your wor: Break must be completely within your work period. Please adjust the break times.
translations.Break end time must be after break start: Break end time must be after break start time.
translations.Break must be at least 15 minutes long t: Break must be at least 15 minutes long to count toward legal break requirements (ArbZG §4). Your break is %s minutes.
translations.Break %s and Break %s overlap. Please ad: Break %s and Break %s overlap. Please adjust the break times so they don't overlap.
translations.Click to quickly set the date to today: Click to quickly set the date to today
translations.You have unsaved changes. Are you sure y: You have unsaved changes. Are you sure you want to leave?
translations.Your session has expired. Please refresh: Your session has expired. Please refresh the page and try again.
translations.Network error or server problem. Retryin: Network error or server problem. Retrying...
translations.Request timed out. Please check your int: Request timed out. Please check your internet connection and try again.
translations.Network error. Please check your interne: Network error. Please check your internet connection and try again. Your data has been saved locally as backup.
Failed to save after multiple attempts. Please check your internet connection and try again.
translations.An error occurred while saving the time: An error occurred while saving the time entry. Please try again.
translations.You are recording work on a Sunday. Sund: You are recording work on a Sunday. Sunday work is generally prohibited under German labor law. Please ensure you have a works council agreement or valid exception. This will be recorded as a warning.
Failed to load users. Please try again.
translations.Failed to load audit logs. Please try ag: Failed to load audit logs. Please try again.
translations.Define teams, departments, or any organi: Define teams, departments, or any organizational units in a flexible hierarchy. Assign members and managers. When enabled below, managers can approve absences and time corrections for their unit members.
translations.Define teams and assign members and mana: Define teams and assign members and managers. Managers can approve absences and time corrections for their team members. When enabled below, this replaces Nextcloud group-based manager resolution.
translations.Use ArbeitszeitCheck teams instead of Ne: Use ArbeitszeitCheck teams instead of Nextcloud groups
translations.When on: managers are taken from the Arb: When on: managers are taken from the ArbeitszeitCheck teams you define below. When off: managers are determined from shared Nextcloud groups (default behaviour).
translations.No units yet. Add a unit to build your o: No units yet. Add a unit to build your organization.
No teams yet. Add a team to get started.
translations.Are you sure you want to delete the unit: Are you sure you want to delete the unit "%s"? Members and managers will be unassigned.
translations.All users are already members of this te: All users are already members of this team
translations.All users are already managers of this t: All users are already managers of this team
translations.User is already a member of this team: User is already a member of this team
translations.User is already a manager of this team: User is already a manager of this team
translations.Additionally send iCal to managers (team: Additionally send iCal to managers (team leads)
translations.selectWorkScheduleHelp: Select the working time model that should apply to this employee. Changes affect future working time and vacation calculations.
translations.vacationDaysHelp: Number of vacation days per year for this working time model or assignment.
translations.endDateHelp: Optional end date for this assignment. Without a date, the model remains valid until changed.
translations.noAssignmentHistory: No working time model assignments have been recorded for this user yet.
translations.Access denied. You can only approve time: Access denied. You can only approve time entries for members of your team.
translations.Access denied. You can only reject time: Access denied. You can only reject time entries for members of your team.
Cannot delete a team that has sub-teams. Move or delete sub-teams first.
translations.CRITICAL: Maximum daily working hours (1: CRITICAL: Maximum daily working hours (10h) exceeded! Automatically clocking out to comply with German labor law (ArbZG §3).
translations.Maximum daily working hours already reac: Maximum daily working hours already reached
translations.This time entry would exceed the maximum: This time entry would exceed the maximum daily working hours of 10 hours (ArbZG §3). Total daily hours would be %s hours.
translations.Mandatory 30-minute break missing after: Mandatory 30-minute break missing after 6 hours of work
translations.Mandatory 45-minute break missing after: Mandatory 45-minute break missing after 9 hours of work
translations.Working hours exceeded 10 hours in a sin: Working hours exceeded 10 hours in a single day
translations.Daily working hours limit reached (10 ho: Daily working hours limit reached (10 hours maximum)
translations.Weekly working hours average limit (48 h: Weekly working hours average limit (48 hours) exceeded
Weekly working hours average limit (48 hours) exceeded over the last 6 months
translations.Minimum 11-hour rest period required bet: Minimum 11-hour rest period required between shifts (ArbZG §5)
translations.Night work detected: %.2f hours between: Night work detected: %.2f hours between 11 PM and 6 AM
translations.Cannot resume: Maximum daily working hou: Cannot resume: Maximum daily working hours (10h) would be exceeded. Current: %.1f hours, would be: %.1f hours (ArbZG §3).
translations.Cannot clock in: Maximum daily working h: Cannot clock in: Maximum daily working hours (10h) already reached. You have already worked %.1f hours today (ArbZG §3).
translations.Onboarding status will be saved after da: Onboarding status will be saved after database migration
translations.A team cannot be its own parent: A team cannot be its own parent
translations.Optional: Record your break times. Germa: Optional: Record your break times. German law requires breaks for work over 6 hours: 30 minutes for 6-9 hours, 45 minutes for 9+ hours. Shorter shifts have no break requirements. Automatic breaks are added when needed for compliance. You can add additional manual breaks.
translations.Optional: When did your break end? Use a: Optional: When did your break end? Use automatic breaks for legal compliance.
translations.Optional: When did your break start? Use: Optional: When did your break start? Use automatic breaks for legal compliance.
translations.Select the day you worked (format: dd.mm: Select the day you worked (format: dd.mm.yyyy, e.g. 15.01.2024). You can also click "Today" to quickly set today's date.
translations.What time did you finish working? (24-ho: What time did you finish working? (24-hour format, e.g. 17:00 for 5 PM. For night shifts, enter the next day's time)
translations.What time did you start working? (24-hou: What time did you start working? (24-hour format, e.g. 09:00 for 9 AM or 17:30 for 5:30 PM)
translations.When enabled, breaks required by German: When enabled, breaks required by German law are automatically added. Disable to manage breaks manually.
translations.See your compliance status and recent pr: See your compliance status and recent problems
translations.See all working time rule violations: See all working time rule violations
translations.No team members in your team. You cannot: No team members in your team. You cannot select a substitute. Add yourself to a team to enable this option.
translations.Log of all actions in the time tracking: Log of all actions in the time tracking system (who changed what and when).
translations.No activities were logged for the select: No activities were logged for the selected period.

translations.Default working hours must be between 1: Default working hours must be between 1 and 24

translations.Hour must be between 0 and 23. You enter: Hour must be between 0 and 23. You entered {hour}.
translations.Minute must be between 0 and 59. You ent: Minute must be between 0 and 59. You entered {minute}.
translations.Break must be at least 15 minutes long (: Break must be at least 15 minutes long (ArbZG §4). Your break is {minutes} minutes.
translations.Break {a} and Break {b} overlap. Please: Break {a} and Break {b} overlap. Please adjust the break times so they do not overlap.
translations.Configure how time tracking and complian: Configure how time tracking and compliance checks work for all employees.
translations.Please try again. If the problem persist: Please try again. If the problem persists, contact your administrator.
translations.For approved absences, an email with an: For approved absences, an email with an iCal attachment (.ics) can be sent automatically.
translations.Send iCal to the person with approved ab: Send iCal to the person with approved absence
translations.Also send iCal to substitute (if selecte: Also send iCal to substitute (if selected)
translations.Also send iCal to managers (team manager: Also send iCal to managers (team managers)
translations.For the selected absence types, a substi: For the selected absence types, a substitute must be designated.
translations.Maximum working hours per day (in hours): Maximum working hours per day (in hours)
translations.Upper limit of daily working time in hou: Upper limit of daily working time in hours. German labor law (ArbZG) allows 8 hours normally, up to 10 in special cases.
translations.Minimum rest period between work days (i: Minimum rest period between work days (in hours)
translations.Hours of rest between end of work and ne: Hours of rest between end of work and next start. German law requires at least 11 hours.
translations.Default daily working hours. Used for ne: Default daily working hours. Used for new employees until individual models are set.
translations.Used for statutory holidays and complian: Used for statutory holidays and compliance when no specific state is configured for employees or teams.
translations.Auto-restore statutory holidays when vie: Auto-restore statutory holidays when viewing calendar
translations.When enabled, missing statutory holidays: When enabled, missing statutory holidays are added when the calendar is viewed. Disable if you want deleted holidays to stay removed.
translations.Data retention period for time records (: Data retention period for time records (in years)
translations.Number of years to keep time tracking da: Number of years to keep time tracking data before automatic deletion (typically at least 2 years).
translations.Save changes and apply to all users: Save changes and apply to all users
translations.Select the federal state whose holiday c: Select the federal state whose holiday calendar applies to this person. If not set, the global default state is used.
translations.This is the last calendar day in each ye: This is the last calendar day in each year when carryover from the opening balance (Resturlaub) may still be used for vacation. You enter each person's opening balance per calendar year under Users. After this date, new vacation requests can only use the annual vacation entitlement from the working time model—not carryover. This applies to everyone.
translations.Only approved vacation counts. For worki: Only approved vacation counts. For working days on or before this date, carryover is used before annual entitlement. Approved absences are applied in chronological order (by start date, then id).
translations.Typical value in Germany: 31 March (mont: Typical value in Germany: 31 March (month 3, day 31). If that day does not exist in a month (e.g. 31 February), the last day of that month is used automatically.
translations.Carryover expiry month must be between 1: Carryover expiry month must be between 1 and 12
translations.Carryover expiry day must be between 1 a: Carryover expiry day must be between 1 and 31
translations.These numbers only count approved vacati: These numbers only count approved vacation. Carryover days must be used for vacation days on or before the expiry date; after that, new requests use your regular annual entitlement first.
translations.Still usable for new requests: %s days: Still usable for new requests: %s days
translations.Carryover from last year: use by %1$s (%: Carryover from last year: use by %1$s (%2$s days still usable for new requests).
translations.Carryover deadline has passed (%1$s). Ne: Carryover deadline has passed (%1$s). New requests can no longer use last year’s remaining days. The opening balance above is your HR record; approved vacation already reduced it.
translations.You still have days left on paper, but t: You still have days left on paper, but they can no longer be booked as carryover—use your regular annual entitlement for new requests.
Admin cap on opening carryover: %s days.
translations.If set, opening carryover per user canno: If set, opening carryover per user cannot exceed this many days (Tarifvertrag / company policy). Leave empty for no cap. Imports and admin edits are clamped to this value.
translations.When enabled, a daily task may copy unus: When enabled, a daily task may copy unused carryover (and optionally unused annual days, see below) into the next calendar year’s opening balance after the carryover deadline, unless a balance already exists for that year. Use the occ command for manual runs.
translations.Include unused annual entitlement in rol: Include unused annual entitlement in rollover (advanced)
translations.Off by default. Only enable if your coll: Off by default. Only enable if your collective agreement allows transferring unused annual leave; consult HR / legal. When on, unused annual days for the year may be added to the next year’s carryover opening, subject to the maximum carryover cap above.
translations.Maximum carryover days must be empty (un: Maximum carryover days must be empty (unlimited) or between 0 and 366
